topic: saudi arabia praises bangladesh hajj management
headline_marked: Saudi Arabia praises [[Bangladesh's Hajj management]] efficiency
headline: Saudi Arabia praises Bangladesh's Hajj management efficiency
summary: Saudi Arabia's Minister of Hajj and Umrah commends Bangladesh's skilled and disciplined Hajj operations this year. He expressed a desire for continued cooperation in future Hajj management.
details: Saudi Arabia's Minister of Hajj and Umrah, Tawfiq bin Fozan Al-Rabiah, has highly praised Bangladesh's efficiency and discipline in managing this year's Hajj pilgrimage. He also expressed a desire for Bangladesh's cooperation in further developing overall Hajj management in the future.

The commendation was made during a bilateral meeting with Bangladesh's Minister of Religious Affairs, Kazi Shah Mofazzal Hossain Kaikobad, held at The Pilgrim's Experience Program Centre in Riyadh. According to a press release from the Ministry of Religious Affairs, the Saudi minister lauded the activities of Bangladesh's Ministry of Religious Affairs for the success of this year's Hajj.

Al-Rabiah stated that Bangladesh's Hajj management this year was highly disciplined and effective. He noted that the skill and coordination demonstrated by Bangladesh in serving the pilgrims were commendable. He also conveyed his sincere thanks to Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina through the Minister of Religious Affairs.

Looking ahead, Al-Rabiah expressed Saudi Arabia's anticipation of Bangladesh's experience and cooperation in further developing and modernizing Hajj management. He informed the Bangladeshi minister about Saudi Arabia's plans and steps taken to make the 2027 Hajj management more advanced and technology-driven.

In response, Minister Kaikobad thanked the Saudi government for the unprecedented development in overall Hajj management. He highlighted that the Saudi government ensured a supportive and safe environment for the pilgrims this year through initiatives like ensuring pilgrim safety, traffic control, crowd management, effective use of information technology, and providing advanced services in the Mashair region.

The Bangladeshi minister extended special gratitude to Saudi King Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud, Crown Prince and Prime Minister Mohammed bin Salman, and Minister Al-Rabiah. He affirmed that Bangladesh would always stand by Saudi Arabia in ensuring the best services for the guests of Allah. Kaikobad expressed hope that the existing fraternal relations between Bangladesh and Saudi Arabia would be further strengthened in the future regarding Hajj management and religious cooperation.

The meeting was attended by the Ambassador of Bangladesh to Saudi Arabia, Mohammad Jilal Uddin, Counselor (Hajj) Md. Kamrul Islam, and other senior officials from the relevant ministries of both countries. Discussions also covered various aspects of Hajj management, future plans, and enhancing bilateral cooperation.
caption:
Saudi Arabia's Hajj Minister praises Bangladesh's 'highly disciplined and effective' Hajj management this year.
He seeks continued cooperation for future improvements.

Saudi Arabia's Minister of Hajj and Umrah, Tawfiq bin Fozan Al-Rabiah, has highly praised Bangladesh's efficiency and discipline in managing this year's Hajj pilgrimage. He also expressed a desire for Bangladesh's cooperation in further developing overall Hajj management in the future.

The commendation was made during a bilateral meeting with Bangladesh's Minister of Religious Affairs, Kazi Shah Mofazzal Hossain Kaikobad, held at The Pilgrim's Experience Program Centre in Riyadh. According to a press release from the Ministry of Religious Affairs, the Saudi minister lauded the activities of Bangladesh's Ministry of Religious Affairs for the success of this year's Hajj.

Al-Rabiah stated that Bangladesh's Hajj management this year was highly disciplined and effective. He noted that the skill and coordination demonstrated by Bangladesh in serving the pilgrims were commendable. He also conveyed his sincere thanks to Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina through the Minister of Religious Affairs.

Looking ahead, Al-Rabiah expressed Saudi Arabia's anticipation of Bangladesh's experience and cooperation in further developing and modernizing Hajj management. He informed the Bangladeshi minister about Saudi Arabia's plans and steps taken to make the 2027 Hajj management more advanced and technology-driven.

In response, Minister Kaikobad thanked the Saudi government for the unprecedented development in overall Hajj management. He highlighted that the Saudi government ensured a supportive and safe environment for the pilgrims this year through initiatives like ensuring pilgrim safety, traffic control, crowd management, effective use of information technology, and providing advanced services in the Mashair region.

#Hajj #SaudiArabia #Bangladesh #Pilgrimage #ReligiousAffairs

Source: Prothom Alo, Dhaka Tribune
tweet: Saudi Arabia commends Bangladesh's highly disciplined and effective Hajj management this year. Minister Tawfiq bin Fozan Al-Rabiah seeks continued cooperation for future improvements. #Hajj #Bangladesh #SaudiArabia

topic: bangladesh budget proposals and financial news
headline_marked: Finance Minister: [[Budget aims to provide relief]] to all Bangladeshis amid limited resources
headline: Finance Minister: Budget aims to provide relief to all Bangladeshis amid limited resources
summary: Bangladesh's Finance Minister Amir Khasru Mahmud Chowdhury presented a budget of Tk 9.38 trillion for the 2026-27 fiscal year, emphasizing economic recovery and investment.
details: The government aims to provide relief to all citizens within the country's limited resources through the upcoming budget, stated Finance Minister Amir Khasru Mahmud Chowdhury on Thursday.

Speaking to reporters before presenting the budget in Parliament, the minister highlighted that the budget reflects the nation's expectations from an elected government, ensuring economic benefits reach everyone.

The new government's first budget, totaling Tk 9.38 trillion for the 2026-27 fiscal year, is set to be presented this afternoon in the National Parliament. The government is striving to guide the economy from fragility towards prosperity.

Expenditures are rising due to global risks, increased spending on subsidies and social safety nets, debt servicing pressures, and the implementation of a new salary structure for government employees. Despite prioritizing economic recovery, investment, and job creation, a record deficit of Tk 2.43 trillion is anticipated.
The budget targets reducing inflation to 7.5 percent in the next fiscal year and projects a GDP growth rate of 6.5 percent.
caption:
Bangladesh unveils a Tk 9.38 trillion budget for 2026-27, prioritizing economic recovery and relief for all citizens.

The government aims to provide relief to all citizens within the country's limited resources through the upcoming budget, stated Finance Minister Amir Khasru Mahmud Chowdhury on Thursday.

Speaking to reporters before presenting the budget in Parliament, the minister highlighted that the budget reflects the nation's expectations from an elected government, ensuring economic benefits reach everyone.

The new government's first budget, totaling Tk 9.38 trillion for the 2026-27 fiscal year, is set to be presented this afternoon in the National Parliament. The government is striving to guide the economy from fragility towards prosperity.

Expenditures are rising due to global risks, increased spending on subsidies and social safety nets, debt servicing pressures, and the implementation of a new salary structure for government employees. Despite prioritizing economic recovery, investment, and job creation, a record deficit of Tk 2.43 trillion is anticipated.

The budget targets reducing inflation to 7.5 percent in the next fiscal year and projects a GDP growth rate of 6.5 percent.

#BangladeshBudget #Economy #Finance #Dhaka #News

Source: Jamuna TV, Dhaka Tribune, The Daily Star
tweet: Bangladesh's Finance Minister presented a Tk 9.38 trillion budget for 2026-27, aiming for economic recovery and relief for all. Key priorities include investment, job creation, and inflation control. #BangladeshBudget #Economy
source: Jamuna TV, Dhaka Tribune, The Daily Star
